description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a roughly cuboidal form and uneven, jagged surfaces. Its exterior is predominantly a dark brownish-gray, with subtle reddish undertones and areas of lighter, weathered texture. One side of the stone appears to have a fractured, layered structure, suggesting it may have broken off from a larger rock formation. The surface is coarse and pitted, with visible micro-texture and occasional small protrusions and indentations. The stone's mass and size make it a formidable obstacle, suitable for use as a barrier or boundary marker in urban environments. There are no visible artificial modifications, carvings, or receptacle features present.
description: A large, rough, dark brownish-gray stone with an irregular, blocky shape and coarse, weathered surfaces. One side shows fractured, layered textures, adding to its natural appearance.
description_view_0: This view highlights the jagged, broken edge and rough, pitted surface, with a dark coloration and visible textural detail.
description_view_1: This side appears smoother and more uniform, with a broad, slightly convex surface and subtle reddish-brown hues.
description_view_2: The fractured, layered structure is most prominent here, showing striations and a more angular profile compared to other views.
description_view_3: This angle reveals a rough, matte surface with irregular bumps and a generally blocky outline, lacking the pronounced layering of view 2.
attribute_location: urban park boundary or roadside verge
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or boundary marker on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and subtle layering, with no artificial carvings or regular patterns; fractured striations visible on one side.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with pitted surfaces and some areas showing signs of natural fracturing and exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural ground cover or landscaping, but can contrast with paved surfaces or man-made structures; may be partially embedded in soil or surrounded by grass.

license_info.creator_display_name: C. Geología de Yacimientos Minerales - FCNyM-UNLP
